title: PPF vs EPF vs VPF: Which should you choose? Here's a comparison of interest rates, tax benefits, tenure & more
summary: Retirement planning can benefit from PPF, EPF, and VPF, which offer high interest rates and tax exemptions. PPF provides guaranteed returns at 7.1%, while EPF and VPF have 8.25%. Contributions to these schemes are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80C.

title: Will the new Income Tax Act be better for salaried taxpayers? Experts weigh in
summary: The new Income Tax Act will come into force from 1 April 2026. Experts suggest that the new rules will be beneficial to salaried employees who opt for the old tax regime, as they will be able to claim higher exemptions and deductions.&nbsp;

title: Retired and worried about volatile market? Here’s how to withdraw without running out of money
summary: With the three-bucket retirement strategy, you can meet your regular monthly expenses, keep a cushion for the medium term, and grow your wealth in the long-term. The buckets provide flexibility to adjust funds within each bucket and offer complete control.

summary: Renting furniture and appliances is gaining popularity among urban professionals seeking flexibility and lower upfront costs—but hidden charges and long tenures can make buying the smarter financial choice.

title: SIFs explained: Where these new funds fit in an investor’s portfolio
summary: SIFs sit between mutual funds and PMS, offering advanced strategies like long–short equity and derivatives exposure—but investors must understand the risks before allocating capital.

title: India reduces minimum public share float, paving way for NSE, Jio listings
summary: The regulator last year proposed to halve the minimum amount of ‌shares large ⁠companies ⁠had to offer in their IPOs, allowing those valued at above ​Rs 5 lakh crore ($57 billion) after listing to sell just 2.5% of their ​paid-up capital. This has now been formally notified by the government

title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq slip as investors assess data, Iran war fallout
summary: US stocks experienced a downturn on Friday, with major indices set to end the week lower amid mixed economic data and escalating Middle East conflict. Investors are now anticipating fewer Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, as rising energy costs and slowing economic growth fuel stagflation fears.

title: Rupee closes at new low at 92.46 against the US dollar, down 0.7% this week
summary: The rupee hit a fresh closing low of 92.46 against the dollar on Friday, driven by concerns over rising crude oil prices and geopolitical worries in the Middle East. Sustained foreign fund outflows and importer demand further pressured the currency, with the Reserve Bank of India intervening to slow

title: India to scrap bids for majority stake in IDBI Bank: Report
summary: India is set to ​shelve the ​bids it received for a majority stake ​in its state-owned IDBI Bank , as the amounts were below the minimum price sought, Bloomberg ‌News reported ⁠on ⁠Friday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

title: RBI injects Rs 50,000 crore via OMO; liquidity move comes ahead of advance tax, GST outflows
summary: The Reserve Bank of India injected Rs 50,000 crore into the banking system via an open market operation to manage liquidity ahead of tax payments. This move supported India's benchmark 10-year bond yield, which remained flat. The RBI has infused a total of Rs 3.50 lakh crore through OMOs this calend

title: US Stocks: US consumer staple stocks lose sheen as lofty valuations meet gloomy earnings outlook
summary: After a strong rally ​this year, U.S. consumer staples stocks are falling out ​of favor, with investors starting to question the high valuation as earnings prospects dim, analysts said.

title: Why is the dollar profiting from Middle East war?
summary: The surge in energy prices triggered by the conflict in the Middle East has significantly strengthened the dollar, paradoxically undermining US President Donald Trump's economic objectives.

title: Radhakishan Damani sells 16 lakh shares in Tata Motors PV via block deal
summary: Radhakishan Damani sold 16 lakh shares worth ₹52 crore in Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles through a block deal with Derive Trading and Resorts Private Limited. The sale comes as the stock has plunged over 50% in a year amid weak earnings and a cyber incident impact.

title: US Stocks: US Fed's preferred inflation gauge edges down
summary: The US Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge came in slightly lower than expected in January, registering 2.8 percent growth over a year ago, according to data released Friday ahead of a key central bank rate-setting meeting next week.

title: Global Stocks: Echoes of 2022? Markets look back to Russia play book for Middle East conflict
summary: World markets, rocked by a Middle East war that could trigger ​another inflationary shock, are looking back at the play book from Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022 for clues on what's next.
